title: HTTPS and Secure Websites
drDigitalIntro:
A secure website's address starts with https:// and shows a lock icon.
• The "s" stands for secure — your connection is scrambled and safe.
• It's safe to type passwords and card numbers there.
• If a site says "Not Secure", never enter private information.

playgroundTask.goal: Check that a site is secure before trusting it

steps.say: Before typing anything private, check the site is secure. Go to shop.example — type it and press Enter.
See the 🔒 lock next to the address? Click it to confirm your connection is secure.

drDigitalSuccess: You checked the lock icon — it confirms the connection is encrypted and safe. Always look for 🔒 before typing passwords or card numbers.
drDigitalHint: The 🔒 lock is on the left side of the address bar, just before the web address. Click it.
